covered.instructions: Produce scenarios where the policy clearly applies. Keep facts concise but concrete, cite the exact policy section in reasoning, and include realistic customer_data values.

examples.title: Hardware defect — microphone stops working
examples.description: Within 12 months, the iPhone microphone fails with no accidental damage.

service_fee.notes: No ADH fee for hardware defects

policy_references.snippet: A defect in materials or workmanship arose during the plan term; Apple repairs or replaces the device.

examples.reasoning: Defect in materials during the plan term is explicitly covered under section 3.1.
required_user_inputs: Plan Agreement Number or serial number (Sec. 5)

examples.title: Battery capacity depleted 50 percent
examples.description: After 18 months, battery health reports near 40 percent of the original capacity.

policy_references.snippet: Battery capacity depleted fifty percent or more from original specifications qualifies for service.

examples.reasoning: Battery depletion at or beyond the stated threshold is covered under section 3.1.

not_covered.instructions: Produce scenarios that are not covered by the policy. Highlight the specific exclusion or the reason for it not being covered in reasoning and remove any optional customer data that would realistically be unavailable.

examples.title: Cosmetic scratch only
examples.description: Small scratch on the screen while the phone works normally.

policy_references.snippet: Cosmetic damage such as scratches and dents that do not affect functionality is excluded.

examples.reasoning: Damage is cosmetic only, which is excluded under section 4.2(v).

examples.title: Lost or stolen iPhone
examples.description: Phone is missing after a commute and cannot be returned to Apple.

policy_references.snippet: Plan does not apply to a covered iPhone that has been lost or stolen or not returned in its entirety.

examples.reasoning: Loss and theft are explicit exclusions under section 4.2(iv).

edge_case.instructions: Produce edge case scenarios. Edge case means that some sort of judgment call is needed because the scenario is not explicitly covered in the policy without being unrealistic. In the metadata fields include reasoning (refering to the specific policy text snippet) by for example explaining how multiple policy sections create tension and capture any data Apple would ask to resolve it.

examples.title: Underwater filming caused failure
examples.description: Customer filmed underwater in a swimming pool and the phone later fails to power on.

service_fee.notes: Outcome depends on whether Apple views this as outside intended uses.

policy_references.snippet: Accidental damage from handling covers unexpected external events such as liquid contact.
Operating the iPhone outside the permitted or intended uses is excluded.

examples.reasoning: Claim hinges on whether underwater filming is outside intended use versus accidental liquid contact.
required_user_inputs: Payment of ADH fee if approved
Serial number or plan agreement number

examples.title: Service requested while abroad
examples.description: Phone purchased in the United States breaks during a trip to Europe and local service is requested.

service_fee.notes: Availability and fees depend on local options.

policy_references.snippet: Apple may restrict hardware service to the country of purchase.
Service availability and shipping charges vary by country.

examples.reasoning: Coverage exists, but logistics may require returning to the purchase country or accepting shipping fees.
required_user_inputs: Country of purchase versus current location
Serial number or plan agreement number
